rationale: Adding CustomOptions to AggregateOptions/ChangeStreamOptions for internal MongoDB cluster feature; API ergonomics improvement, not spec nonconformance.

BSON unmarshalling allocates value for null pointer when custom unmarshaler present; Go-specific behavior, no named spec rule violated.

TLS HandshakeContext improvement to avoid goroutine leak; internal implementation improvement, no spec rule violated.
Docs/spec define empty readPreferenceTag as fail-safe; driver fails instead of treating empty tag as match-all document.

Issue split from DRIVERS-2033; Improvement implementing new loadBalanced=True hello+OP_MSG requirement, not fixing wrong existing behavior.
Tests expected errors on capped collection deletes but server changed behavior in 5.3; test maintenance for server version compatibility.

Serverless tests failing because capped collections banned; test skip for platform incompatibility, no spec behavior involved.
pool.Put() always releases semaphore even for nil/foreign connections; implementation bug, CMAP spec has no specific rule about this guard.

Issue split DRIVERS-1804; CMAP spec requires connection pools created/ready for any server in direct connection; driver violated this rule.

Adding new-style Go 1.17 build tags to source files; code formatting/toolchain maintenance, no spec involvement.

Test fails because server changed behavior (count added to API v1); test maintenance for server version change, not driver nonconformance.
Refactoring state constants for connection/pool/server/topology types; internal code quality improvement, no spec rule involved.
CMAP spec defines idle as time since available in pool; driver only refreshed deadline on read/write, violating spec definition.

Refactoring test code to use per-test pool monitors instead of global; test code quality improvement, no spec behavior.
Adding license header check to Makefile/Evergreen; CI/build tooling, no spec involvement.
updateOne response 'n' field type mismatch int32 vs int64 since 1.7.5; regression in wire protocol decoding, but no specific spec rule names the type.

User reports unexpected second write op; investigation suggests user-side issue or separate process; insufficient evidence of driver spec violation.

Moving uuid package to internal to prevent misuse; API encapsulation refactor, no spec involvement.

Removing unused x/bsonx POC API from production code; internal code cleanup/refactor, no spec behavior change.
